name: azure-ai-projects-dotnet
description: Azure AI Projects SDK for .NET. High-level client for Azure AI Foundry projects including agents, connections, datasets, deployments, evaluations, and indexes.
capabilities: Guide creation of persistent agents with code interpreter and file search tools
Explain versioned agent patterns using Azure.AI.Projects.OpenAI preview package
Demonstrate connection, deployment, and dataset management workflows
Provide evaluation configuration examples for AI model assessments
Show Azure AI Search index creation and management patterns
Explain authentication with DefaultAzureCredential for production scenarios
author: sickn33
securitySummary: This is a documentation-only skill providing guidance for Azure AI Projects SDK for .NET. Static analysis found 0 security issues with a risk score of 0/100. The skill contains code examples that follow Azure security best practices including DefaultAzureCredential for authentication and async patterns for I/O operations. No executable code, network calls, or suspicious patterns detected.
valueStatement: Developers struggle to integrate Azure AI Foundry services into .NET applications. This skill provides complete SDK guidance for agents, connections, datasets, and evaluations with production-ready patterns.
userTitle: Build AI agents with Azure AI Foundry for .NET
limitations: Requires Azure AI Foundry project and appropriate Azure subscriptions
Some features like versioned agents require preview packages
Does not execute code - provides documentation and guidance only
Code examples target .NET 8+ and Azure SDK beta versions

useCases.title: Enterprise AI Agent Development
useCases.description: Build production AI agents with persistent threads, tool integration, and versioned deployments for enterprise applications.

useCases.targetUser: ML engineers and data scientists validating AI model performance
useCases.title: AI Model Evaluation Pipeline
useCases.description: Create automated evaluation workflows for AI models using built-in evaluators for relevance, fluency, and groundedness metrics.

useCases.targetUser: Solution architects designing knowledge-aware AI systems
useCases.title: RAG Solution with Azure AI Search
useCases.description: Implement retrieval-augmented generation solutions connecting AI agents to Azure AI Search indexes for knowledge-based responses.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Agent Setup
promptTemplates.prompt: Help me create a simple persistent agent using Azure.AI.Projects SDK. I need an agent that can answer customer support questions using the gpt-4o-mini model. Show me the authentication setup and basic agent creation code.
promptTemplates.scenario: Beginner - Creating your first Azure AI agent

promptTemplates.title: Agent with File Search
promptTemplates.prompt: I want to create an agent that can search through uploaded documentation files. Show me how to configure the FileSearchToolDefinition, upload a dataset, and attach it to a persistent agent in Azure AI Foundry.
promptTemplates.scenario: Intermediate - Adding retrieval capabilities to agents

promptTemplates.title: Versioned Agent Deployment
promptTemplates.prompt: Explain how to use Azure.AI.Projects.OpenAI package to create versioned agents. I need to deploy an agent with web search tools, get the response client, and manage agent versions for production rollout.
promptTemplates.scenario: Advanced - Production agent versioning and deployment

promptTemplates.title: Evaluation Framework Setup
promptTemplates.prompt: Help me set up an evaluation pipeline for my AI agent. Show me how to configure the Relevance evaluator, create an input dataset, run the evaluation, and interpret the results using the EvaluationsClient.
promptTemplates.scenario: Advanced - AI model evaluation and quality assurance

bestPractices: Use DefaultAzureCredential for production authentication to leverage managed identities and avoid hardcoded credentials
Always use async methods for I/O operations and poll with 500ms delays when waiting for agent runs to complete
Clean up resources by deleting threads, agents, and datasets when no longer needed to avoid unnecessary costs
antiPatterns: Do not use includeCredentials: true unless you specifically need credential values - this exposes sensitive information
Avoid synchronous methods in production code as they block threads and reduce scalability
Do not store agent or thread IDs in client-side code without proper validation and access controls

faq.question: What is the difference between AIProjectClient and PersistentAgentsClient?
faq.answer: AIProjectClient is the main entry point for all Azure AI Foundry operations. GetPersistentAgentsClient() returns a specialized client for low-level agent operations including thread management and run execution.

faq.question: Do I need preview packages to use Azure AI Projects SDK?
faq.answer: The core SDK (Azure.AI.Projects) is generally available. Preview packages like Azure.AI.Projects.OpenAI and Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent are only needed for advanced features like versioned agents and low-level operations.

faq.question: How do I authenticate to Azure AI Foundry from my .NET application?
faq.answer: Use DefaultAzureCredential which automatically tries multiple authentication methods including managed identities, Azure CLI credentials, and environment variables. This is the recommended approach for production deployments.

faq.question: Can I use Azure AI Projects SDK with existing OpenAI code?
faq.answer: Yes, you can get an AzureOpenAIClient from the project client and use standard OpenAI chat completions. The SDK provides GetConnection and GetProjectResponsesClientForAgent methods for OpenAI integration.

faq.question: What tools are available for Azure AI agents?
faq.answer: Available tools include Code Interpreter for Python execution, File Search for document queries, Bing Grounding for web search, Azure AI Search for custom indexes, OpenAPI for external APIs, Azure Functions, and MCP for Model Context Protocol.

faq.question: How do I handle errors when calling Azure AI Foundry APIs?
faq.answer: Wrap API calls in try-catch blocks and handle RequestFailedException. Check the Status code, ErrorCode, and Message properties to determine the appropriate retry or fallback behavior.
